clues.clue: 'Who lives in a pineapple under the sea?' opens this Nickelodeon series.
'Sunny Day, sweepin' the clouds away' opens this long-running PBS children's program.
'A horse is a horse, of course, of course' introduced this 1960s talking-equine sitcom.
'Come and listen to a story about a man named Jed' opens this rags-to-riches sitcom.
'Just sit right back and you'll hear a tale' opens this 1960s castaway sitcom.
This mischievous pet monkey accompanies the street rat in 'Aladdin.'
This Jamaican-accented crab serves King Triton in 'The Little Mermaid.'
Voiced by Eddie Murphy, this small red dragon guards the heroine of 'Mulan.'
In 'Cinderella,' name the plump mouse befriended by Jaq.
Princess Jasmine's fiercely protective pet tiger bears this name.
This yellow sea sponge works as a fry cook at the Krusty Krab.
This pink starfish is SpongeBob's dim-witted best friend.
On 'The Wild Thornberrys,' young Eliza has the secret power to do this with animals.
This Hanna-Barbera Great Dane solves mysteries with a teen gang in the Mystery Machine.
This cat-and-mouse duo headlined a slapstick MGM theatrical-short rivalry from 1940.
Rolling these two pip-marked cubes governs movement in many games.
In Monopoly, landing on the wrong corner sends a player to this place.
In Scrabble, a tile on a dark-red premium square multiplies a word's value by this factor.
In poker, three of a kind combined with a pair makes this hand.
In spades or bridge, a player's pledge to take a number of tricks is called this.
This last son of Krypton lives on Earth as reporter Clark Kent.
This Amazonian princess wields a golden Lasso of Truth.
This trident-bearing monarch commands the seas from Atlantis.
This emerald, Robin Hood–styled archer is the millionaire Oliver Queen.
Boy Billy Batson shouts a magic word to become this hero, once called Captain Marvel.

final.clue: Rod Serling's narration invited viewers into 'another dimension' on this 1959 sci-fi anthology.
